Defendants Notice Of Motion For An Order Awarding An Evidentiary Sanction For Plaintiffs Failure To Comply With A Prior Court Order
Off Calendar Sep-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ROBERT KWONG'S Motion For An Order Awarding An Evidentiary Sanction For Plaintiff's Failure To Comply With A Prior Court Order as follows: Defendant's motion for an order imposing evidentiary sanctions specified in the notice of motion is unopposed and granted. The plaintiff has failed to comply with this Court's Order dated April 24, 2017 to respond to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Request for Production. The order to be prepared by the moving party shall specify the evidentiary sanctions. The Court notes sanctions in the sum of $267.00 which were ordered to be paid within thirty days of April 24, 2017 have not as yet been paid. Plaintiff is again ordered to pay said sanctions within ten days. A copy of this order shall be served with Notice of Entry. Defendant's request for additional sanctions in connection with the within motion is denied as sanctions are not requested in the notice of motion. Moving party to submit a proposed order. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Plaintiff is again ordered to pay said sanction within ten days. A copy of this order shall be served with Notice of Entry. Defendant's request for additional sanctions in connection with the within motion is denied as sanctions are not requested in the notice of motion. Moving party to submit a proposed order.
